summary: DHH announces the release of TextMate 1.0, a text editor for OS X, expressing excitement about delivering on the hype that had built up around it. He draws a parallel to his previous successful launches of Basecamp and Rails, positioning TextMate as his third major release. He tempers expectations while highlighting that the editor aims to enhance both productivity and enjoyment for OS X users.
key_points: TextMate 1.0 has launched after significant anticipation, marking DHH's third major product release alongside Basecamp and Rails
DHH acknowledges that hype inevitably leads to some disappointment but frames expectations around productivity and enjoyment rather than magic
The emphasis on enjoyment over pure productivity reflects DHH's recurring philosophy that developer tools should be pleasurable to use
money_quotes: It feels incredibly good to release TextMate after all the anticipation that has built up around it.
I've been so lucky as to have this be my third experience of the kind. First Basecamp, then Rails, and now TextMate.
Undoubtedly, all this hype will lead to a few disappointed faces.
No, TextMate won't make you breakfast in bed. Or complete your over-due project for you.
But it will hopefully enhance your productivity, and most importantly, your enjoyment in working with a graphical editor on OS X.
key_insight: The best developer tools should prioritize enjoyment alongside productivity, and managing expectations honestly is part of delivering on hype.
